methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $18M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Dendra Systems is a Series A-stage climate and energy company that has raised $18M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of three same-sector, same-stage private companies—Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M)—alongside stage-progression methodology applied to frontier-tech venture outcomes.

Two valuation methods were triangulated. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $18M raised as a proxy for current value, yielding $36M. Peer-median analysis uses the three comparable companies, which span $110M to $200M with a median of $150M, reflecting market pricing for similar-stage Climate & Energy assets. The peer range reflects significant heterogeneity in execution, capital efficiency, or technical de-risking across this nascent cohort.

The triangulated analysis implies a valuation range of $31M (floor, representing a modest step-up scenario) to $172M (ceiling, weighted toward peer-median upside), with a median estimate of $93M. This wide band reflects genuine uncertainty: stage-progression and peer comparables diverge materially, suggesting either that Dendra's capital deployment efficiency or technical standing differs materially from peers, or that the peer set itself exhibits early-stage valuation volatility. Key sensitivities include traction on core metrics not provided here, capital efficiency relative to the peer cohort, and macro conditions affecting frontier-energy fundraising. A point estimate within the $80M–$130M range appears most defensible pending deeper operational diligence.
